quiz_questions.question_text: On a position-time graph, what does a horizontal line represent?

options.text: The object is accelerating
The object has zero position
The object has constant velocity
The object is at rest

quiz_questions.explanation: A horizontal line on a position-time graph means the position is not changing over time, which indicates the object is stationary.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, what does the slope represent?

options.text: Speed
Acceleration
Velocity
Displacement

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a velocity-time graph equals the rate of change of velocity, which by definition is acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a position-time graph, what does the slope represent?

options.text: Distance
Velocity
Acceleration
Force

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a position-time graph equals Δx/Δt, which is the definition of velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, what does the area under the curve represent?

options.text: Displacement
Velocity
Force
Acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: The area under a velocity-time graph equals ∫v dt, which gives displacement. This follows directly from the definition of velocity as dx/dt.

quiz_questions.question_text: A straight line with a positive slope on a position-time graph indicates:

options.text: The object is at rest
Increasing acceleration
Decreasing velocity
Constant positive velocity

quiz_questions.explanation: A straight line means constant slope, which means constant velocity. A positive slope means the object moves in the positive direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: A curved line on a position-time graph indicates:

options.text: The object is at rest
The object has constant velocity
The object is accelerating
The object has negative velocity

quiz_questions.explanation: A curve means the slope (velocity) is changing over time, which is the definition of acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a straight line passing through the origin on a velocity-time graph represent?

options.text: Decreasing speed
Zero acceleration
Constant acceleration from rest
Constant velocity

quiz_questions.explanation: A straight line through the origin on a v-t graph has the form v = at (where a is constant), meaning constant acceleration starting from rest (v₀ = 0).

quiz_questions.question_text: On a velocity-time graph, a line below the time axis indicates:

options.text: The object is at rest
The object has stopped
Motion in the negative direction
Positive acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: Negative velocity values on a v-t graph mean the object is moving in the negative direction (opposite to the chosen positive direction).

quiz_questions.question_text: On an acceleration-time graph, a horizontal line at a = 0 means:

options.text: The object is speeding up
The object has infinite speed
The object has constant velocity (zero acceleration)
The object is at rest

quiz_questions.explanation: Zero acceleration on an a-t graph means velocity is not changing — the object moves at constant velocity (which could be zero or nonzero).

quiz_questions.question_text: How do you find the instantaneous velocity from a position-time graph?

options.text: Draw a tangent line at that point and find its slope
Find the area under the curve
Calculate the total distance traveled
Read the y-value directly

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous velocity equals the slope of the tangent line to a position-time graph at a specific point, representing the derivative dx/dt at that instant.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes a position-time graph?

options.text: A graph that only works for objects at rest
A graph showing the speed of an object over distance
A graph that plots an object's position along the vertical axis against time along the horizontal axis, showing how location changes over time
A chart comparing position to acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: A position-time (x-t) graph has time on the horizontal axis and position on the vertical axis. Its slope gives velocity, and its curvature indicates acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes a velocity-time graph?

options.text: A graph where the y-axis represents position
A graph that shows only speed, not direction
A graph that plots velocity on the vertical axis against time on the horizontal axis, where the slope gives acceleration and the area gives displacement
A graph that can only show constant motion

quiz_questions.explanation: A velocity-time (v-t) graph displays how velocity changes with time. The slope at any point gives acceleration, and the area between the curve and time axis gives displacement.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes instantaneous velocity?

options.text: The velocity at the start of motion only
The velocity of an object at a single specific moment in time, found as the slope of the tangent to the position-time curve at that instant
The total distance divided by total time
The fastest speed an object reaches

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous velocity is the limit of Δx/Δt as Δt approaches zero — it is the derivative of position with respect to time at a particular instant.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes average velocity?

options.text: The total displacement divided by the total time interval, represented by the slope of the secant line between two points on a position-time graph
The area under a velocity-time graph
The speed at the midpoint of a journey
The highest velocity reached during a trip

quiz_questions.explanation: Average velocity = Δx/Δt. On a position-time graph, it equals the slope of the straight line (secant) connecting the initial and final points.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes the tangent line on a graph?

options.text: The x-axis of any graph
A horizontal line at the peak of a curve
A straight line that touches a curve at exactly one point and has the same slope as the curve at that point
A line that crosses through a curve at two points

quiz_questions.explanation: The tangent line at a point on a curve represents the instantaneous rate of change (slope) of the function at that point. In physics, it gives instantaneous velocity or acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes a secant line on a graph?

options.text: A line parallel to the x-axis
A line tangent to the curve at one point
A straight line that intersects a curve at two points, with its slope representing the average rate of change between those points
A vertical line on a graph

quiz_questions.explanation: A secant line connects two points on a curve. Its slope gives the average rate of change between those two points — for a position-time graph, this is the average velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes the area under a curve (in kinematics)?

options.text: The maximum value on the graph
The integral of the graphed quantity over time, which gives the next kinematic variable — area under v-t gives displacement, area under a-t gives change in velocity
The total energy of the system
A measure of how curved the line is

quiz_questions.explanation: In kinematics graphs, the area under the curve relates consecutive variables: area under v-t = displacement, area under a-t = Δv. This is the graphical interpretation of integration.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes uniform motion on a graph?

options.text: Motion represented by a straight line on a position-time graph and a horizontal line on a velocity-time graph, indicating constant velocity and zero acceleration
Motion where the object always returns to its starting point
Motion with a curved position-time graph
Any motion shown on a graph

quiz_questions.explanation: Uniform motion means constant velocity. On an x-t graph this appears as a straight line (constant slope), and on a v-t graph as a horizontal line (constant value, zero slope = zero acceleration).

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes non-uniform motion on a graph?

options.text: Motion in a straight line at constant speed
Motion where the acceleration is always constant
Motion represented by a curved line on a position-time graph and a non-horizontal line on a velocity-time graph, indicating changing velocity
Motion that cannot be represented on a graph

quiz_questions.explanation: Non-uniform motion involves changing velocity. On an x-t graph the line curves (changing slope), and on a v-t graph the line is not horizontal (acceleration ≠ 0).

quiz_questions.question_text: Which definition BEST describes an acceleration-time graph?

options.text: A graph identical to a velocity-time graph
A graph that only shows constant acceleration
A graph that plots acceleration on the vertical axis against time on the horizontal axis, where the area under the curve gives the change in velocity
A graph where the slope gives position

quiz_questions.explanation: An acceleration-time (a-t) graph shows how acceleration varies with time. The area under the curve between two times gives the change in velocity (Δv) during that interval.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student has a position-time graph showing a straight line from (0 s, 0 m) to (5 s, 20 m). What is the velocity?

options.text: 4 m/s — the slope of the line is Δx/Δt = (20 − 0)/(5 − 0) = 4 m/s
20 m/s
5 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Velocity = slope of the x-t graph = Δx/Δt = 20 m / 5 s = 4 m/s. The constant slope indicates uniform velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: A velocity-time graph shows a horizontal line at v = 8 m/s from t = 0 to t = 6 s. What is the displacement?

options.text: 48 m — displacement = area = base × height = 6 × 8 = 48 m
8 m
6 m

quiz_questions.explanation: Displacement = area under v-t graph = rectangle area = v × t = 8 m/s × 6 s = 48 m.

quiz_questions.question_text: A velocity-time graph shows a straight line from (0 s, 0 m/s) to (4 s, 12 m/s). What is the acceleration and displacement?

options.text: Acceleration = 12 m/s², displacement = 48 m
Acceleration = 3 m/s², displacement = 48 m
Acceleration = 3 m/s², displacement = 24 m — slope = 12/4 = 3 m/s², area of triangle = ½ × 4 × 12 = 24 m
Acceleration = 4 m/s², displacement = 12 m

quiz_questions.explanation: Acceleration = slope = 12/4 = 3 m/s². Displacement = area of triangle = ½ × base × height = ½ × 4 × 12 = 24 m.

quiz_questions.question_text: A position-time graph shows a parabolic curve opening upward. At t = 3 s, the tangent line has a slope of 6 m/s. What can you determine?

options.text: The average velocity is 6 m/s
The instantaneous velocity at t = 3 s is 6 m/s, and the object is accelerating because the graph is curved
The acceleration is 6 m/s²
The object is decelerating

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of the tangent to an x-t graph at a point gives the instantaneous velocity at that time. The parabolic shape (changing slope) indicates non-zero acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car's velocity-time graph shows v = 10 m/s from t = 0 to t = 3 s, then v increases linearly from 10 m/s to 25 m/s between t = 3 s and t = 8 s. What is the total displacement?

options.text: 117.5 m — Phase 1: 10 × 3 = 30 m; Phase 2: trapezoid area = ½(10 + 25)(5) = 87.5 m; Total = 30 + 87.5 = 117.5 m

quiz_questions.explanation: Phase 1 (rectangle): 10 × 3 = 30 m. Phase 2 (trapezoid): ½(10 + 25) × 5 = 87.5 m. Total = 30 + 87.5 = 117.5 m.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two cars have different position-time graphs. Car A has a steeper straight line than Car B. What does this tell you?

options.text: Car A has greater acceleration
Car A is accelerating more than Car B
Car B will eventually pass Car A
Car A has a greater constant velocity than Car B — a steeper slope on a position-time graph means faster movement

quiz_questions.explanation: On a position-time graph, the slope gives velocity. A steeper slope means a larger velocity. Since both are straight lines, both have constant velocities, with Car A moving faster.

quiz_questions.question_text: An object's velocity-time graph is a straight line from (0 s, 20 m/s) to (5 s, 0 m/s). Describe the motion.

options.text: The object moves at constant velocity of 20 m/s
The object decelerates uniformly at 4 m/s² from 20 m/s to rest, covering 50 m — acceleration = (0 − 20)/5 = −4 m/s², displacement = ½(20 + 0)(5) = 50 m
The object reverses direction at t = 5 s
The object accelerates from rest to 20 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Slope = (0 − 20)/5 = −4 m/s² (deceleration). Displacement = area of triangle = ½ × 5 × 20 = 50 m. The object uniformly decelerates from 20 m/s to rest in 5 seconds.

quiz_questions.question_text: A velocity-time graph shows a line going from +10 m/s at t = 0 to −10 m/s at t = 4 s. At what time does the object momentarily stop, and what is the total displacement?

options.text: It never stops; displacement = 20 m
It stops at t = 2 s; displacement = 20 m
It stops at t = 4 s; displacement = 40 m
The object stops at t = 2 s when the line crosses zero; displacement = 0 m because the positive and negative areas cancel

quiz_questions.explanation: The line crosses v = 0 at t = 2 s (by symmetry or linear interpolation). Positive area (0 to 2 s): ½ × 2 × 10 = 10 m. Negative area (2 to 4 s): ½ × 2 × (−10) = −10 m. Net displacement = 10 + (−10) = 0 m.

quiz_questions.question_text: A physics teacher draws an acceleration-time graph showing a constant value of 3 m/s² from t = 0 to t = 6 s. If the initial velocity is 2 m/s, what is the final velocity?

options.text: 18 m/s
3 m/s
6 m/s
20 m/s — the area under the a-t graph gives Δv = 3 × 6 = 18 m/s, so v = 2 + 18 = 20 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Area under a-t graph = Δv. Area = 3 m/s² × 6 s = 18 m/s. Final velocity = v₀ + Δv = 2 + 18 = 20 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student plots a position-time graph and observes the line curves steeply, then flattens. The student concludes the object accelerated then moved at constant velocity. A classmate says the object accelerated then decelerated. Who can determine who is correct from the graph?

options.text: Both are wrong — curves only show constant velocity
The classmate, because all curves represent deceleration

The student — a steep curve flattening to a straight line (constant slope) indicates acceleration followed by constant velocity, while a curve flattening to horizontal would indicate deceleration to rest
Neither — you need a velocity-time graph to tell

quiz_questions.explanation: If the x-t curve transitions from increasing slope to constant (nonzero) slope, velocity went from changing to constant — acceleration then constant velocity. If it flattens to zero slope (horizontal), the object decelerated to rest. The shape of the curve distinguishes the two interpretations.
